name: Babu-Mohapatra parity solution to strong CP
summary: Solves the strong-CP problem by promoting parity to an exact symmetry of the underlying theory, broken only spontaneously or softly. Above the parity-breaking scale theta = 0 by symmetry. After parity breaking, theta_eff is generated only through CP-violating operators in the mass matrices and stays naturally small. A common realisation lives within left-right symmetric models with a generalised seesaw for quark masses.

addresses_puzzles.note: theta_eff vanishes up to one-loop in the minimal Babu-Mohapatra construction; radiative regeneration at higher orders has to be checked model by model. Modern analyses (e.g., de Vries et al. 2021, arXiv:2109.01630) test how robust the cancellation is across realistic constructions.

touches_benchmarks.note: Loop-induced contributions to theta_eff and quark chromo-EDMs sit close to current bounds; nEDM upgrades will further constrain or rule out specific implementations.

new_fields: additional Higgs structure to break parity
introduces.note: Parity-breaking scale and Higgs sector parameters; the structural role of the symmetry constrains what would otherwise be many free parameters.

history: Babu and Mohapatra (1989); 'A Solution to the Strong CP Problem Without an Axion', Phys. Rev. D 41, 1286 (1990). Periodically revived; the 2021 analysis of de Vries-Murgui-Stoffer-Walker-Loud (arXiv:2109.01630) tested the robustness of minimal parity solutions and found them tightly constrained but not closed.

references.citation: Babu and Mohapatra, 'A Solution to the Strong CP Problem Without an Axion', Phys. Rev. D 41, 1286 (1990)
de Vries, Murgui, Stoffer, Walker-Loud, 'Do Minimal Parity Solutions to the Strong CP Problem Work?' (2021)
